refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To be atomic and solution-neutral, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle. This refinement focuses on the load phase as one atomic functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, ensuring the p95 cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load mechanism speed and efficiency
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback
Design load phase process to minimize idle and transition times
Allocate sufficient power and torque resources to meet cycle time targets
Integrate load phase operations with upstream and downstream processes for seamless flow
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design load phase process to minimize idle and transition times

potential_sources: Interdependence between load and travel phases
Feedback loops from cycle time monitoring to load mechanism control
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on minimizing idle and transition times within the load phase itself, which supports independence from other phases and allows modular optimization. Potential coupling with travel and dump phases exists but can be managed through clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time target is achievable given physical and operational constraints
Real-time monitoring and control systems are available to measure and adjust cycle times
Operational policies and safety plans do not impose constraints that significantly increase load phase duration
Variability in site conditions is within manageable limits
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP that addresses real-time monitoring and adaptive control to ensure cycle time targets are met under variable conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic and coordination processes, including load phase process design to optimize operational efficiency.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends operational status and performance data during load phase.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives and manages task assignments related to load phase scheduling and execution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with external modules and systems during load phase.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as load phase process is a core system function; minimal diffusion as it coordinates with other modules via defined interfaces.
Partial containment since vehicle executes tasks but does not design or optimize load phase process; high diffusion due to reliance on external coordination.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control rather than process design; high diffusion expected.
Minimal containment; safety services monitor and intervene but do not manage load phase process; high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the design load phase process DP because it centralizes the coordination and optimization logic, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some load phase logic is embedded in vehicle control software.
Dependency on external interfaces may introduce coupling that complicates module independence.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Design load phase process to minimize idle and transition times' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase by focusing on process optimization to reduce delays, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of meeting p95 cycle time targets under specified conditions.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some load phase logic is embedded in vehicle control software.
Dependency on external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) may introduce coupling that complicates module independence.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment score (8) for the DP, centralizing coordination and optimization logic, which minimizes diffusion risk. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, indicating that the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as well as ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear, with defined interfaces for telemetry, tasking, and network communication supporting the load phase process design and execution.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document any load phase related logic embedded in the Vehicle module to reduce overlap and diffusion risk.
Review and manage dependencies on external interfaces to maintain module independence and reduce coupling.
Consider adding explicit safety monitoring interfaces or coordination points with Safety Services to ensure operational policies are integrated without increasing diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating that the current level of decomposition is sound and well-contained within the NGHE Solution module. However, given the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks related to overlap with the Vehicle module and dependencies on external interfaces, further decomposition of the FR into sub-FRs is warranted to explicitly address these concerns. This will help clarify responsibilities, reduce coupling, and integrate safety monitoring more explicitly, thereby mitigating risks and improving verification and allocation clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize idle and transition times during load phase
Isolate load phase logic from Vehicle control software
Manage dependencies on Telemetry, Tasking, and Network Link interfaces
Integrate safety monitoring and coordination with Safety Services

revise.why: To explicitly capture sub-functions that address identified diffusion risks and safety integration, ensuring clearer allocation and verification at the next level.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary implementation boundary for the load phase process.
External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) are necessary but pose coupling risks.
Safety criticality requires explicit functional decomposition to ensure compliance and monitoring.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion with Vehicle module logic.
Coupling due to dependencies on external interfaces.
Ensuring safety policies are integrated without increasing diffusion risk.
evidence_needed: Documentation of load phase logic in Vehicle module to clarify overlap.
Interface specifications and dependency management plans for external systems.
Verification plans demonstrating safety monitoring integration and cycle time compliance.
